text:
CORRYTON, Tenn. (WVLT) - Out of 101 athletes in the United States competing in the upcoming Special Olympics World Winter Games,only threeare from East Tennessee.

Among those three Tennesseans competing in Italy, Johnny Kelly is the only one from East Tennessee who will be representing his country.

“It’s just cool because it’s my home country and state,” said Kelly.

Kelly isn’t new to competing in the Special Olympics, he’s done it for nearly a decade but in 2022 he took home three gold medals and a silver in powerlifting during the USA games in Florida.

The 25-year-old Gibbs High School graduate works out every day at the YMCA in Powell as he prepares for his snowboarding competition on the world’s stage in 2025.

“Running more for cardio because constantly moving on the board, falling, pushing, getting back up definitely is a lot more tiresome than what powerlifting can be because it’s one lift and you’re done,” said Kelly.

While Tennessee doesn’t get a lot of snow, Kelly finds his time to prepare by going to Ober Gatlinburg when snowboarding is open for him to go. He also said he watches videos on how to maximize his speed going down the courses in an effort to go for gold once again.

“Just kind of like hard work paid off, and kind of like a stress off of your shoulders,” said Kelly about winning gold in 2022.

Kelly said this trip to Italy will be the first time he’s ever left the country, as he’s admittedly a little hesitant about the long travel it’ll take.

He said he’s most enjoyed the friends he’s made over his years competing in the Special Olympics as he looks forward to his next one in March of 2025.

title: Johnny Kelly will compete in snowboarding  in the 2025 Special Olympics.
description: Johnny Kelly will compete in snowboarding in the 2025 Special Olympics.
keywords: Special Olympics,Special Olympic World Winter Games,snowboarding,powerlifting,olympics,East Tennessee,Gibbs,Johnny Kelly,Italy,Tennessee
